page: Vultr vhf-12c-48gb is a High Frequency Compute server featuring 12 vCPUs and 12 physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Core Processor based on the Skylake architecture. The CPU operates at 2.0 GHz with a shared allocation model and one thread per core, supported by 32 KB L1d cache, 4 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 48.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 768 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU hardware is present on this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 25%. Single-threaded gzip compression and PassMark memory scores reach the top 10% tier. Conversely, multi-core performance, static web serving, and database operations fall into the average category. A notable performance bottleneck is large-block memory bandwidth, where both read and write speeds rank in the bottom 25%, alongside weak medium-model LLM inference speeds.

This server represents a resource-dense option for workloads that benefit from high single-thread execution speeds. The shared CPU allocation provides a cost-effective hardware profile for general-purpose applications, web serving, and moderate database operations, though it is less suited for memory-intensive large-block data processing or heavy multi-threaded parallel workloads.
description: Vultr vhf-12c-48gb is a High Frequency Compute server configured with 12 shared Intel Skylake vCPUs, 48.0 GB of RAM, and 768 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It delivers strong single-core performance, highlighted by top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and strong single-core stress-ng benchmarks. However, it exhibits average multi-core performance and weak large-block memory bandwidth. This hardware profile makes it a cost-effective choice for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and moderate database tasks that prioritize single-thread speed over sustained multi-core parallel processing or high-throughput memory operations.
og_description: Vultr vhf-12c-48gb is a High Frequency Compute server with 12 shared Intel vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and 768 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance but weak large-block memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-12c-48gb features 12 shared Intel vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and 768 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: A shared-CPU server featuring strong single-core performance, balanced memory, and fast NVMe storage for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 12 shared Intel Skylake vCPUs and 48.0 GB of system memory
768 GB of local NVMe SSD storage included
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and strong single-core benchmarks
Tradeoff of weak large-block memory read and write bandwidth
Suitable for web serving, moderate database tasks, and general-purpose applications
